conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children. I'm inquiring about all of them - Alex who is 3, Blake who is 8, and Charlie who is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have three children: Alex (3 years old), Blake (8 years old), and Charlie (2 years old).

Now, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great. Now I need to ask about your responsibility for these children.

Are you responsible for all three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie? By this I mean, do they all live with you, and are you responsible for their care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. All three of them live with me and I'm responsible for their care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about any other adults who might also be responsible for the children.

Is there anyone else (such as a partner, spouse, or another adult) who also lives with you and shares responsibility for caring for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, it's just me caring for them at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are any of the children - Alex, Blake, or Charlie - currently in local authority care, or do any of them spend time living with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in care and they all live with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

Are Alex, Blake, and Charlie all under 16 years old? (I can see from the ages you've provided that they are 3, 8, and 2, but I just need to confirm there are no other circumstances that might affect their status, such as being in approved education or training if they were 16-19.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they're all under 16 - Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 2.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children: Alex, Blake, and Charlie.**

Here's the breakdown:

**Alex (3 years old):** ✓ Eligible
- Under 16 years old
- Lives with you
- You are responsible for their care
- Not in local authority care

**Blake (8 years old):** ✓ Eligible
- Under 16 years old
- Lives with you
- You are responsible for their care
- Not in local authority care

**Charlie (2 years old):** ✓ Eligible
- Under 16 years old
- Lives with you
- You are responsible for their care
- Not in local authority care

You meet all the requirements: you live in the UK, you're responsible for the children, they all live with you, and they're all under 16 years old.

You can apply for Child Benefit for all three children. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant who is responsible for their care, resides in the UK, and is not in local authority care. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant who is responsible for their care, resides in the UK, and is not in local authority care. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant who is responsible for their care, resides in the UK, and is not in local authority care. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is solely responsible for three children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2). All children are under 16, live with the claimant full-time, and are not in local authority care. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for all three children.
